select_result.selected_heading: NVIDIA Cosmos 3 — The Open Model That Thinks in the Physical World
select_result.reason: This story has a clear and exciting narrative—a groundbreaking open-source model (Cosmos 3) that can understand the physical world, with a bold claim from Jensen Huang. It's surprising, potentially impactful, and translates well to a conversational tone. The phrase 'thinks in the physical world' is instantly engaging and doesn't require complex visuals to understand. It's more emotionally resonant than the other sections and avoids getting bogged down in benchmarks.
select_result.hook: Forget AI that just *sees* the world – NVIDIA just unveiled an open-source model that can actually *understand* how it *works*.

clips.heading: Cosmos 3 - Hook
clips.script: Imagine an AI that *understands* the real world, not just pixels on a screen. NVIDIA just released a model aiming to do exactly that. Stay tuned.

clips.heading: Cosmos 3 - Key Feature
clips.script: Cosmos 3 is the first fully open ‘omnimodel.’ It processes text, images, video, sound *and* understands physics. A huge leap for AI robotics.

clips.heading: Cosmos 3 - Wrap
clips.script: NVIDIA claims Cosmos 3 dramatically speeds up physical AI development. Jensen Huang predicts a 'big bang' is coming. This changes everything.

select_result.selected_heading: NVIDIA Cosmos 3 — The Open Model That Thinks in the Physical World

clips.heading: Conclusion
clips.script: Physical AI's big bang is near
